relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Empire issues a decree over the inhabited world.
The registration is placed in imperial time.
Everyone is moved toward ancestral city.
Joseph goes from Nazareth to Bethlehem, David’s city.
Mary travels pregnant within the registration.
Her birth-time is fulfilled there.
The firstborn is wrapped and placed in a manger where no guest-room place was found.
Shepherds keep night-watch in the fields.
Glory surrounds them and fear rises.
Fear is answered by good news of great joy for all the people.
A Savior, Messiah, Lord is born in David’s city.
The sign is a baby in cloths and a manger.
Heaven’s army joins the praise.
Glory above and peace on earth are sung together.
The shepherds go to see the word-event made known to them.
They find Mary, Joseph, and the baby in the manger.
Seeing becomes witness.
Those hearing the shepherds marvel.
Mary keeps and ponders the words/events in her heart.
The shepherds return in praise for what they heard and saw.
On the eighth day he receives the name Jesus.
The family brings him to Jerusalem according to Moses’ Law.
The first opening the womb is called holy to the Lord.
The offering reveals humble means.
Simeon waits for Israel’s consolation with holy Spirit upon him.
Spirit reveals he will see the Lord’s Messiah before death.
Spirit brings him into the temple at the moment Jesus is brought.
He receives the child and blesses the Divine.
Now the servant may be released in peace.
His eyes have seen salvation.
This salvation is prepared before all peoples.
Light for the nations and glory for Israel meet in the child.
His parents marvel at the words.
Simeon blesses and names falling, rising, and sign of contradiction.
Mary’s soul will be pierced as hearts are revealed.
Anna the prophetess stands in long faithfulness.
Her widowhood becomes temple service in fasting and prayer.
She gives thanks and speaks to those awaiting Jerusalem’s redemption.
After fulfilling the Law, they return to Nazareth.
The child grows strong, filled with wisdom and favor.
Each year his parents go to Jerusalem for Passover.
At twelve, Jesus joins the feast journey.
He remains in Jerusalem after the feast days.
His parents seek him among the travel group.
Not finding him, they return to Jerusalem.
After three days, they find him in the temple listening and questioning.
His understanding astonishes the hearers.
Mary speaks the distress of seeking him.
Jesus names the necessity of being in his Father’s things.
They do not yet understand the word.
He returns to Nazareth in hidden obedience while Mary keeps the words/events in her heart.
Jesus advances in wisdom, stature, and favor with the Divine and humans.
